title: Talk the Talk Day!
story: People across Europe celebrate how amazing it is to speak different languages on September 26! On this special day, schools organize language fairs where kids learn to say "hello" in dozens of tongues. Libraries host storytelling sessions in multiple languages, and communities put on cultural festivals featuring food, music, and traditions from different countries. Many people challenge themselves to learn a few words in a new language or practice one they're studying. Social media fills up with videos of people showing off their language skills!
activity_question: What do schools often organize on European Day of Languages?
follow_up_question: How many different languages do you think are spoken in Europe?

title: Love Note Day: Sweet Messages Everywhere!
story: People around the world grab their pens and get creative on September 26 for Love Note Day! This sweet holiday encourages everyone to write heartfelt messages to the people they care about. Kids slip notes into their parents' lunch boxes, friends exchange colorful cards at school, and families leave surprise messages on bathroom mirrors or tucked inside books. Some people get fancy with glitter pens and stickers, while others keep it simple with sticky notes. The best part? You don't need expensive gifts – just kind words that make someone smile on this special day each year.
activity_question: What do people use to write love notes on Love Note Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most creative place you could hide a love note for someone to find?

title: Johnny Appleseed's Apple-solutely Amazing Day
story: Every September 26, people across America celebrate National Johnny Appleseed Day by honoring the legendary folk hero who planted apple trees everywhere he went! Schools organize apple-themed activities like bobbing for apples, making applesauce, and reading stories about Johnny's adventures. Many families visit apple orchards, bake apple pies, or plant their own apple trees in their yards. Some communities hold festivals with apple cider, caramel apples, and contests for the biggest or tastiest apple varieties. It's a sweet way to remember how one person's simple idea of planting seeds grew into a tradition that still feeds people today!
activity_question: What do schools often organize on National Johnny Appleseed Day?
activity_choices: Apple-themed activities like bobbing for apples
activity_answer: Apple-themed activities like bobbing for apples
follow_up_question: What kind of tree would you want to plant to help future generations?
